channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Franceine Jhobie Rosario breaks Palarong Pambansa pole vault record
item.description: In the 2026 Palarong Pambansa held at the Datu Lipus Makapandong Governor Democrito O. Plaza Sports Complex in Agusan del Sur, Franceine Jhobie Rosario of the National Capital Region showcased her res...

item.title: Kenneth Law expected to plead guilty to assisting suicide as murder charges are withdrawn
item.description: Kenneth Law, an Ontario man accused of selling lethal substances online to individuals who later died by suicide, is expected to plead guilty in a Newmarket, Ontario courtroom. According to court deve...

item.title: 19th-Century Russian Kokoshnik Headdress and Its Cultural Significance
item.description: The article explores the historical and cultural significance of the kokoshnik, a traditional Russian women’s headdress widely associated with late 19th-century fashion and earlier centuries of Slavic...

item.title: Factors That Can Block Your Prayers from Being Answered
item.description: Many people in Tanzania and beyond sometimes wonder why their prayers no reach the way dem expect. Religious leaders explain say there are several spiritual and personal factors wey fit block prayers ...

item.title: Senator Jinggoy Estrada faces arrest over alleged flood control corruption
item.description: The Sandiganbayan’s 2nd Division has issued an arrest warrant against Senator Jinggoy Estrada over a graft case linked to flood control projects, marking him as the highest-ranking incumbent official ...

item.title: DOJ Issues Subpoenas to Identify Anonymous Social Media Users Criticizing ICE
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice (DOJ) has escalated efforts to uncover the identities of anonymous social media users who have criticized immigration enforcement, specifically the operations of Immigra...

item.title: Letters Highlight Varied Opinions on Steven Guilbeault’s Climate Approach and Canadian Politics
item.description: The Globe and Mail’s letters to the editor for May 29 feature a range of perspectives on climate policy, Canadian politics, and governance. Several contributors commented on former Environment Ministe...

item.title: Customs files criminal charges against Makati Express Cargo for leaving thousands of balikbayan boxes unclaimed
item.description: MANILA, Philippines – The Bureau of Customs (BOC) has officially filed criminal complaints against Makati Express Cargo Incorporated (MECI), a cargo company based in Cavite, for abandoning a significa...

item.title: Labor disputes property industry modelling on impact of tax reforms on rents and housing supply
item.description: Federal Health Minister Mark Butler has rejected forecasts from property industry groups claiming that the Albanese Government’s proposed tax reforms will significantly worsen outcomes for renters and...

item.title: Origin experience set to boost Canberra Raiders ahead of crucial Cowboys clash
item.description: Canberra Raiders players Hudson Young and Ethan Strange are expected to bring renewed energy and confidence back to the club after playing key roles in New South Wales’ State of Origin victory. Both p...

item.title: International King’s Cup football and entertainment event set for South African debut
item.description: The internationally recognised King’s Cup Soccer event is set to expand into South Africa, bringing a large-scale football and entertainment showcase to local fans. The tournament combines exhibition ...

item.title: President summons NA and Senate budget sessions on June 5 for FY2026-27 discussions
item.description: President Asif Ali Zardari has formally summoned separate sessions of the National Assembly and the Senate on June 5 to deliberate on the federal budget for the fiscal year 2026-27. According to an of...

item.title: London to host 2026 Ballon d’Or ceremony marking first return outside France in years
item.description: France Football don announce say London go host di 2026 Ballon d’Or awards ceremony, wey go happen on October 26. Dis na di first time London go host di prestigious football award in im 70-year histor...

item.title: Two charged over fatal shooting of innocent bystander in Sheffield nightclub incident
item.description: Two people have been charged in connection with the fatal shooting of a 30-year-old mother who was killed while out in Sheffield city centre. The victim, identified as Shanice Brookes, was described b...

item.title: President Zardari outlines initiatives for national stability and economic growth
item.description: President Asif Ali Zardari, during his visit to Moro city in Naushahro Feroze district, called for united efforts to strengthen Pakistan’s federation and economic stability. He emphasised that the gov...

item.title: NDC Confirms Nationwide Primaries Today, Only Winning Aspirants to Pay Fees
item.description: The Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC) don yan say dem go hold all levels primaries today across di country for Presidential, National Assembly, Governorship, and State Assembly positions as per dia or...

item.title: Russian messaging app Max launched in Tanzania to expand African user base
item.description: A Russian-developed messaging application known as Max has been officially launched in Tanzania as part of its broader expansion strategy across Africa. The launch took place in Dar es Salaam during a...

item.title: Mama June’s gradual efforts to rebuild strained relationships with her family in Georgia
item.description: Mama June Shannon, the American reality TV personality known for her role in 'Mama June: Family Crisis', is currently based in Georgia, USA, where she is trying to rebuild her fractured family relatio...

item.title: Utumishi Girls Academy Fire Claims Life of Heroic Student
item.description: The nation is still mourning the devastating fire that occurred at Utumishi Girls Academy in Gilgil, Nakuru County, which claimed the lives of 16 students and left 79 others injured. Among those who p...

item.title: Concerns over youth e-bike misuse and responsibility in Canberra suburbs
item.description: The opinion piece discusses growing concerns in Canberra about groups of young people using electric bikes and small motorbikes to cause disruption in suburban areas. The author describes incidents wh...
